title: Reign
description: Professions, trade, kingdoms and vassalage. Become a king or serve your lord.
body:
Translated to on **[ ENG | RU ]** lang. The mod is in _active development_. There may be bugs. If you want to take part in the test, read the **[developer diary](https://t.me/reignmod)** and you can play in our **[test-server](https://t.me/reignserver)** [ru] ![TextBook](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bdca4367c7b3c5bb22c2234848b37048eeada0d7.png)
#
This mod adds a profession system, market trading, vassalage with a kingdom distribution system, as well as gradual vanilla minecraft upgrade. You can be a king, lord, knight or freeman!

![From chaos to regularity](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/aff90be5ff2b04af9587839c883b925ca7d01ec7.png)

# 🛠️ SYSTEM OF PROFESSIONS
The player can choose a primary and secondary license (profession). There are 5 primary and 5 secondary licenses. To open the license selection window, press the **[J]** button.
![Licenses_list](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/05b76fed4f6c1059ca81664c843cffd9bbeb8218.png)

<details>
<summary>List of licenses</summary>

## Main licenses:
### Woodcutter  ![woodcutter](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ae626ba85bf96dbfc5b4284c1890218c62f825dc.png)
- Can cut down trees without penalties. Trees cut down to the roots fall whole.
- Supplies logs and coal to the market.
### Smith  ![Blacksmith](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/581a459918d3f0bbfd7510fd8f74efa17f3db539.png)
- Creates armor and tools (better than stone ones) for all other professions on the workbench, while other players will craft almost broken items.
- Repairs tools on the grindstone.
- Delivers tools and armor to the market.
### Miner  ![miner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9d723f1c95bb83801240865dad8539354c14480b_0.webp)
- Extracts ores, but other players do not get resources from the ore.
- Can break deep rocks.
- Delivers ores to the market.
### Farmer   ![farmer](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/80ee3134085b69937539f50e55d99fd83caad07d.png)
- Grows crops and breeds livestock.
- Supplies food to the market.
### Cowboy   ![cowboy](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8dacff1aa9f446206900a6f8d9de299f63702f39.png)
- Breeds cattle.
- Breaking in horses
- Supplies meat and by-products to the market.
#
_Each profession is inextricably linked with others. For example, a blacksmith, in order to create a pickaxe, will need to buy iron from a miner and coal from a woodcutter. A miner, in order to effectively extract ores, needs that same pickaxe from a blacksmith. And food. Everyone needs food._
## Additional licenses:
### Fisherman  ![fisher](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/731ae5699b0f23e31afa3c1502d9f1eab5be4519.png)
- Has the ability to catch fish, gets more experience from fishing.
### Enchanter  ![enchanter](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c710b096e18c2194f1e833f7314b0da0b275987a_0.webp)  
- Can use the enchantment table.
### Alchemist  ![alchemist](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/274e803c4bb30bb82e3fe6e9f72054cd141b6f96.png)
- Creates potions in the alchemy stand, other professions do not know how to prepare potions.
### Soldier  ![soldier](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/129d918a830731511405c0792ffa071da2574d54.png)
- Has increased combat characteristics and survivability.
### Hunter  ![hunter](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7531ef588beef01f5acf80367bc4e3c9eee91a20_0.webp)
- Obtains more drops from wild and hostile creatures.
#
_Each license has its own level progress [0 - 5 lvl]. To level up each level of the main profession, the player's vanilla experience is required. At any time, the player can abandon his profession, but in this case, he loses all level progress. Each day of real time, the player can abandon the profession 1 time._

</details>



# 🛠️ MARKET SYSTEM
There are 4 types of coins: copper, silver, gold and platinum. The more expensive coin is worth 16 of the previous ones. The center of the entire economy is the capital - all the necessary blocks are located here. As long as there is money in the treasury, the capital lands remain protected and under the King’s rule.
![coins_list](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/df5618c1baac1ec38822f5a6c00e763b04b7814b.png)

<details>
<summary>Capital Blocks</summary>

### Trader Point
- This is a block that exchanges player resources for coins. All items sold to the reception point are sent to the market as goods for other players. Selling prices are static. If there are too many items of one type on the market, the reception point stops buying that type of goods.
### Market
- ​​In this block you can buy goods sold by other players for coins. The price is subject to a tax determined by the King.
### Treasury
- Coins earned through taxation are sent here. Only the King has access to the treasury.
### Hoarder
- This block can exchange 64 units of any junk for 1 copper coin.
### King table
- This block is used to issue licenses, rental agreements, and contracts.
### Private shop
- This block allows buying and selling items at a private price.
### Rental block
- Allows granting a player rental rights in the capital, where they can build. To do this, place the rental contract inside.
#
_Also, each player gets some coins for daily login. The amount of coins you get depends on your title._

</details>


# 💎 WORLD EVOLUTION
At the start of the game, many things are unavailable. In order to develop the world, you need to upgrade **Eras**. Each new Era opens access to more valuable resources, licenses and worlds. There are 10 stages in total, divided into 3 eras - **Early, High and Late Middle Ages.**
![Eras_list](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5e0457f0ceea381f955ad734f32995686b3b30d2.png)
### Found 
- This is a block in the center of the capital. With the help of donations of platinum coins, you can develop the world to the next era. Each next level requires more coins.
# ⚜️ HOUSES AND VASSALITY
Players are divided by titles, depending on their role in the kingdom. At the start of the game, you can create up to 4 Houses, but as the era progresses, you can increase the maximum number to 8.

<details>
<summary>Titles in the Kingdom</summary>

### Lords 
- Guardians of the Houses. The player who created the House is the Lord and has a claim to the throne.
### Knights 
- Vassals of the Lord who has joined his House and created domain. To join the House, the Lord must initiate the future knight. To do this, the player must bow his head to the Lord, remove everything from his hands and crouch (Shift).
### Serf
- Serves a knight (or a Lord) and belongs to a Domain.
### Freemen
- Players who are not members of a House. Free.
#
_Houses are divided into domains, which are privatized territories with a suspicion system for outsiders. If an intruder causes trouble on another's land, they will gain suspicion points, and the domain owner will be able to track this, recognizing them by face. If the criminal crosses the line, they will automatically be declared a fugitive for the entire House._


</details>

<details>
<summary>Positions at Court</summary>

## Positions at Court
### King ![crown](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/d6e5083a09ddddca700e89647ce786e26c1a4a5b.png) 
- A Lord with a crown on his head. Rules the entire kingdom, sets taxes, and appoints court officials.
The King's duties include maintaining order in the capital and ensuring its upkeep.
A rebellion against the King can be triggered if the treasury runs dry and the source in the capital fund begins to boil.

### Right Hand  
- The Right Hand – The King’s most trusted Lord, empowered to act in his name and handle all royal affairs. Only a fellow Lord may hold this title.
### Marshal
- A knight or lord of the court who oversees the pursuit of players, has access to the dungeon, and maintains order in the kingdom.
### Treasurer
- Has access to the treasury and can manage taxes.
### Architect
- Can build within the capital’s protected zone, improving and beautifying the area.
### Clerk
- Works with documents and helps with real estate rentals in the capital.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Commands for titles</summary>

## Commands for titles:
- Declare/pardon a criminal (for the Royal Pursuit)
```
/criminal <Player> wanted (or) cancel
```
- set prison (for the King)
```
/prison set
```

## Commands for Houses (Lords):
- View wanted players list:
```
/house wanted
```
- Houses list:
```
/houses
```
- Declare/pardon a criminal (for the House Pursuit)
```
/house criminal (or) pardon <Player>
```
## Commands for Domains (Knights):
- View suspects players list:
```
/domain suspects
```
</details>


# ⚙️ INSTALLATION AND SETUP
Move the downloaded file to the **/mods** folder.
### Setting up a world
To start the game, create a New world. You will need a moderator with **/op** rights.
Choose a suitable location for the **capital** and enter the command to create the capital:


<details>
<summary>Commands for admin</summary>

```
/reign capital set
```
- Create new crown (ID++):
```
/king create
```
- For the test, you can set the desired Era of the world..
```
/reign era set (value)
```
_Each crown has its own ID. If the crown was lost, the moderator can create a new one with this command. Then the previous crown will lose its value._
#
The mod has a built-in guide that appears once you install [Patchouli](https://modrinth.com/mod/patchouli) ![TextBook](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bdca4367c7b3c5bb22c2234848b37048eeada0d7.png), with lots of interesting content! 

</details>

The mod has a built-in guide that appears once you install [Patchouli](https://modrinth.com/mod/patchouli) ![TextBook](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bdca4367c7b3c5bb22c2234848b37048eeada0d7.png), with lots of interesting content!
